column4: NASA LaRC THERMIC transient test on one PEEK plate; instrumented 7.9 mm diameter by 12.7 mm plugs; Type K thermocouples; 1D Crank-Nicolson model; Continuous Genetic Algorithm fit using all three center-plug thermocouples
column5: k(T)=c0+c1*T+c2*T^2+c3*T^3 in W/(m K); c0..c3=-4.0607e-2,1.8749e-3,-4.4753e-6,3.9027e-9; strict admitted range 300-525 K at source-described atmospheric pressure; Results and Abstract state 300-525 K while Concluding Remarks says 300-550 K, so the narrower repeated range governs; four thermocouple-subset estimates differed by about 3 percent, but this is not a stated confidence interval; chosen all-thermocouple fit was about 35 percent below prior laser-flash values; source does not identify PEEK grade, supplier, crystallinity, filler, or processing history

column4: NASA LaRC differential-scanning-calorimeter measurements fitted as the known specific-heat input to the PEEK thermal model
column5: cp(T)=1.0477e-5*T^3-6.1332e-3*T^2+3.3066*T+3.3606e2 in J/(kg K); the paper does not label Equation 2 inline, but J/(kg K) is the SI unit required by Equation 1's dimensional balance with k in W/(m K), density in kg/m3, x in m, and t in s; exact-point evaluations are admitted only over the thermal model's stricter 300-525 K range; source reports no residual, dispersion, confidence level, degrees of freedom, PEEK grade, supplier, crystallinity, filler, or processing history

column4: Commercial-laboratory density measurement used as the known constant-density input to the NASA PEEK thermal model
column5: NASA reports 1264 kg/m3 and cites Netzsch report 621004797 dated July 2019; test temperature, pressure, method details, dispersion, confidence metadata, PEEK grade, supplier, crystallinity, filler, and processing history are not reported
